title: brain purge (--note <id> | --source <id> | --data-category <cat>) [--apply] [--export-challenge | --authorization <path>] — --json success output
description: Privileged irreversible erasure of everything matching the selector across every storage class. Default-SAFE: a bare invocation is a non-mutating PREVIEW that resolves the selector to an immutable erasure inventory (inventoryId + digest) and prints it — it NEVER erases. Erasure requires an explicit --apply; --dry-run is the explicit preview and combining --dry-run with --apply is a usage error (exit 5). The selector is EXACTLY ONE of --note/--source/--data-category (zero or more than one ⇒ exit 5) — there is no opaque positional selector. On --apply the broker challenge is bound to the complete authorization tuple (op purge, oldHead, the deterministically derived replacementHead, scope, nonce, expiresAt, and the immutable inventory snapshot identity inventoryId+inventoryDigest) per security-broker-contract.md §7.4/§12.2; the broker performs the signed non-fast-forward canonical-ref replacement, then per-storage-class erasure with resumable checkpoints, tombstones, projection+index rebuild, and post-purge verification proving no matching copy survives. Authorized ONLY by an OS-presence assertion bound to the challenge or the non-interactive --export-challenge → sign → --authorization flow; --yes never authorizes. Without an authorization on --apply, exits 6 (action-required) and, with --export-challenge, emits an AuthorizationChallenge (security-broker-contract.md §7.1) instead of this success object.

allOf.description: An applied purge always erases + verifies and carries the broker challengeBinding + its erasureClass (security-broker-contract.md §12).

Ordinary erasure (§12.1) performs NO canonical-ref rewrite — the opaque-id ↔ natural-id map row is deleted and a signed tombstone audit event is appended, so no append-only-history rewrite is required. refReplaced is false and the challenge is bound to scope + inventory (no oldHead/replacementHead).

Content-in-history erasure (§12.2) is the single history-rewrite exception: the broker performs a SIGNED non-fast-forward canonical-ref replacement (oldHead → deterministically derived replacementHead), then securely GCs the superseded objects and WORM-checkpoints both heads. refReplaced is true and the challengeBinding MUST carry oldHead + replacementHead.

mode.description: preview = default-safe resolved inventory only, nothing erased; applied = erasure performed under --apply + broker authorization.

erasureClass.description: Which erasure protocol the applied purge used (security-broker-contract.md §12): ordinary = opaque-id map deletion + signed tombstone, NO canonical-ref rewrite (the common GDPR-class case); history-rewrite = the single exception where erasable content is in canonical git history, requiring the broker's signed non-fast-forward canonical-ref replacement. Present on mode=applied.

refReplaced.description: Whether the canonical ref was rewritten: false for ordinary erasure (no ref rewrite), true for history-rewrite (signed non-fast-forward replacement).

scope.description: The single selector the run was scoped to (exactly one of the three kinds).

value.description: The selector value (note id, source id, or data-category name).

inventoryId.description: Immutable id of the resolved erasure-inventory snapshot; the challenge is bound to this identity so the applied erasure matches exactly the previewed set.

inventoryDigest.description: Content digest of the immutable inventory snapshot the challenge was bound to.

erasedClasses.description: Per-storage-class counts: the resolved inventory to erase (mode=preview) or actually erased (mode=applied).

class.description: Storage class name (e.g. blob-store, sqlite, lancedb, ledger-projection).

count.description: Number of objects in that class.

verified.description: Mandatory completion postcondition on mode=applied (const true): post-purge verification confirmed no matching copy survives in any storage class. false on a preview (nothing erased/verified).

challengeBinding.description: The authorization binding the applied purge was verified against (security-broker-contract.md §7.4/§12). The broker rejects any drift between these and re-derived state before acting. oldHead + replacementHead are present ONLY for erasureClass=history-rewrite (the signed non-fast-forward replacement, §12.2); ordinary erasure (§12.1) rewrites no ref and omits them.

oldHead.description: History-rewrite only: the canonical head the challenge was bound to (must equal the canonical tip at verify time; drift ⇒ authz.canonical_moved).

replacementHead.description: History-rewrite only: the deterministically derived post-purge canonical head (broker re-derives and matches; mismatch ⇒ authz.target_mismatch).

scope.description: The selector scope bound into the challenge (substituting a different scope ⇒ authz.target_mismatch).

inventoryId.description: The immutable inventory snapshot identity the challenge was bound to (inventory drift ⇒ authz.target_mismatch).

inventoryDigest.description: The inventory snapshot digest the challenge was bound to.

x-atlas-contract.summary: Default-safe preview by default; --apply performs broker-authorized (oldHead+replacementHead+scope+nonce/expiry+inventory-snapshot-bound) irreversible erasure across every storage class with resumable checkpoints, tombstones, projection+index rebuild, and post-purge verification.

flags.description: Selector: erase everything for this note. Exactly one of --note/--source/--data-category must be supplied.
Selector: erase everything derived from this source. Exactly one selector required.
Selector: erase everything in this data category. Exactly one selector required.
Perform the erasure (requires broker authorization). Without it, emit a non-mutating preview of the resolved inventory.

With --apply: emit the broker AuthorizationChallenge (op purge, bound to oldHead + deterministic replacementHead + scope + nonce/expiry + inventory snapshot identity) to stdout and exit 6 instead of purging; sign it out of band with an enrolled approver key.
Path to a signed AuthorizationResponse produced from a prior --export-challenge; presents it to the broker to authorize the purge.

Caller-supplied idempotency key; a repeat --apply with the same key + request-hash (requestHashScope) returns the existing completed purge rather than re-erasing (registry idempotency: key-accepting).

sideEffects: preview: resolves the selector to an immutable erasure inventory (printed); no erasure, no ref write
apply (ordinary, §12.1): delete the opaque-id ↔ natural-id map row(s) + append a signed erase.tombstone audit event; NO canonical-ref rewrite
apply (history-rewrite, §12.2): broker signed non-fast-forward canonical-ref replacement (oldHead → deterministic replacementHead) + secure GC of superseded objects + WORM checkpoint of both heads
apply: per-storage-class erasure with resumable checkpoints
apply: post-purge verification across every storage class
prohibitedEffects: a bare invocation NEVER erases — erasure requires explicit --apply plus broker authorization
no opaque positional selector — exactly one of --note/--source/--data-category
no prohibited copy survives in any storage class after a verified purge
--yes never authorizes; never bypasses the broker challenge
never runs without the vault-maintenance exclusive lock

errorCodes.when: unknown flag/argument, not exactly one of --note/--source/--data-category, or --dry-run combined with --apply
the selector resolves to an empty inventory (nothing to purge)
broker drift: oldHead is no longer the canonical tip (security-broker-contract.md §7.3)
broker drift: re-derived replacementHead, scope, or inventory identity differs from the challenge
broker: the challenge nonce expired (expiresAt passed)
broker: the OS presence assertion is absent or not bound to the challenge
broker: signingPayload differs from the broker-recomputed canonical bytes
broker: the challenge/response fails the contracts schema (universal broker validation failure, security-broker-contract.md §7.3)
broker: the response's payloadCanonicalization id is unknown (universal broker validation failure, security-broker-contract.md §7.3)
broker: signerId is not in the signer registry
broker: the signer is not permitted for this op
broker: the nonce was not issued by this broker
broker: the nonce was already consumed
the fail-closed backup watermark blocks a ledger-writing run
unexpected internal failure during erasure or verification
